**Applicant Tracking System** (ATS) is a software program designed to automate, simplify and fasten the entire recruiting process. Vast benefits of using an Applicant Tracking System are best demonstrated by its ability to improve the three most important hiring metrics: time-to-hire, cost-per-hire and quality-of-hire.

## What is an Applicant Tracking System

An **Applicant Tracking System** (ATS) is a software program or tool designed to **automate**, **streamline** and **improve** the **hiring** process.

Applicant Tracking System offers a wide range of **solutions for** different **hiring challenges**, from **sourcing** to **testing** and **selecting** the best candidates.

Applicant Tracking System provides an **automated** way for HR professionals to **simplify and fasten** the entire recruiting process, from receiving applications to hiring employees.

## Features and benefits of an ATS

So, what exactly can an ATS do for you?

A modern and well-designed Applicant Tracking System can help you achieve recruiting goals and overcome challenges by offering the following **features** :

* Powerful sourcing tool
* One-click job posting to multiple job boards
* A central repository of applicants’ résumés and applications
* An easily searchable database of rich applicants’ profiles
* Automated resume parsing and screening
* Easy interview scheduling
* Interview kits and scorecards
* Ready to use HR templates
* Automated email responses
* Easy team collaboration and communication
* Recruiting analytics and reporting

**Get a more extensive checklist of all the features a modern recruiting software should have!**

Using all these Applicant Tracking System features provides numerous **benefits** , including:

* Speeding up the recruitment process
* Lovering recruiting costs
* Improving the quality of hire
* Elevating candidate experience
* Building high-quality talent pools
* Developing stronger relationships with candidates
* Advancing decision making
* Upgrading HR team communication and collaboration

The use of the Applicant Tracking System has been proven to advance the **most important hiring metrics** : time-to-hire, cost-per-hire and quality-of-hire.

## ATS benefit #1: Shorter time-to-hire

### What is time-to-hire?

One of the **most important** metrics for recruitment is **time-to-hire**. Time-to-hire is the **total time** spent to fill an open job position.

Time-to-hire **calculation** includes different activities like creating a job posting, job posting promotion, sourcing, interviewing and finally hiring candidates.

This key recruitment metric shows the **efficiency** of the recruitment process and provides insight into the difficulty of filling a certain job position.

### How does an ATS improve time-to-hire?

According to research, **86% of recruiting professionals** say that using an ATS has helped them **hire faster**.

Applicant-tracking-system-benefit-time-to-hire

With the Applicant Tracking System, tasks that previously required many hours of work can now be executed very quickly and easily – or sometimes even done automatically. Some of these tasks include: resume parsing, disqualifications of non-qualified applicants, interview scheduling, and email automation.

Applicant Tracking System also saves time by building talent pools, which can be used to find perfect candidates for your future roles and job openings.

## ATS benefit #2: Lower cost-per-hire

### What is cost-per-hire?

The second most important recruitment metric is **cost-per-hire**. Cost-per-hire is the **total amount of monetary investment** needed to recruit new employees.

Cost-per-hire is **calculated** based on different variables, including recruiter salaries, employee referral bonuses, job advertising costs, travel and relocation costs, etc.

This recruitment metric shows the **cost-effectiveness** of the recruitment process and helps you **allocate** your hiring **budget** based on insights about the most and least expensive job positions.

### How does an ATS improve cost-per-hire?

Since time is money, Applicant Tracking System saves money simply by **saving** your precious **time**. With many administrative recruiting tasks being automated, you need **fewer human hours** to fill a position, which makes your hiring strategy **more productive** and **cost-efficient**.

Also, building and maintaining a **talent pool** can do wonders for your cost per hire. Imagine having a perfect job candidate **every time** when a new position opens!

In addition, having insights into which **source of hire** is bringing you the best new hires (a feature that ATS offers), can help you eliminate unnecessary expenses. For example, if you know that referred candidates are your best new hires, there is no need for spending hundreds or even thousands of dollars on job boards.

## ATS benefit #3: Better quality-of-hire

### What is quality-of-hire?

Another crucial recruitment metric is the **quality of hire**. Quality of hire is the total value that new employees bring to a company with their performance.

Cost-per-hire is **calculated** based on different variables, including performance reviews, ramp-up time, turnover and retention rates, hiring manager satisfaction etc.

This recruitment metric shows the effectiveness of the recruitment process and measures your **assessment accuracy**.

### How does an ATS improve the quality of hire?

Research shows that **78% of recruiter** s who use an ATS have **improved the quality of candidates** they hire.

Applicant-tracking-system-benefit-quality-of-hire

According to research and every recruiter and HR professional everyday work experience, high-quality candidates stay available on the market for **10 days only! 
**By speeding up your recruitment process you can get those top candidates before they get snatched up by another company.

Also, by sending **automated emails** which ensure regular and **timely communication** with your candidates you can significantly **improve** your **candidate engagement** and **candidate experience** .

According to different research and studies ( CareerBuilder Survey, 2013; 2014, 2015 and 2016 Candidate Experience Reports etc.), candidates who had a **positive candidate experience** in your recruiting process will more likely **accept** your job offer, **reapply** in the future and **refer** other high-quality candidates to your company.

## Should I invest in Applicant Tracking System?

**To make this decision easier, get our ultimate guide for buying an ATS!**

As you can see, Applicant Tracking Systems offer **many different solutions** for various recruiting goals and challenges.

Therefore, **buying the right one** is not an easy task.

If you are planning on investing in a new recruiting tool, you should start by **i d entifying your recruiting goals and challenges** .

What are your **most pressing** recruiting challenges? 
Do they include any of the following **recruiting challenges**?

* Long time-to-hire
* High cost-to-hire
* Low quality-of-hire
* Inability to source people with good skills and characteristics
* Extensive use of Excel spreadsheets and emails
* No centralized, searchable database of all the candidates and applicants
* Messy recruiting and selection process
* Manual and time-consuming interview scheduling
* No applicant’s interview kits, assignments, and scorecards
* Poor and slow communication with candidates
* Manual rejection and feedback to candidates
* No strategy for building talent pools
* Not using previous applicants (silver medalists) for future openings
* Inability to evaluate and measure the effectiveness of your recruiting strategy

If so, you could definitely **benefit** from investing in a modern and well-designed **Applicant Tracking System.**

**The applicant tracking system** will make your hiring strategy **more productiv** e and **cost-efficient** by helping you organize and track applications, manage talent pools and streamline your selection process.
